forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,43 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KVTV89QPMT2Z63H2KZ8BJ3M1
+- 01KVTV8TDX7RVS3Q91ZS05QB30
+- 01KVTV96NPVW5RXGRX8KRPB70R
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a580
+project: null
+title: edit files — cascade application (anchor + literal ladder), atomic batch, idempotency
+---
+## What
+Replace the sequential `content.matches`/`replacen` apply logic in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/edit/mod.rs` with the shape-inferred cascade over the canonical `Vec<(find, replace)>` produced by the normalization task. Wire in `swissarmyhammer-hashline` and `swissarmyhammer-edit-match` (add both to `swissarmyhammer-tools` deps).
+
+Per pair, run the cascade on `find`:
+1. If `find` parses as a hashline anchor (`N:HH` or `N:HH|text`) **and resolves** (line exists and hashes to HH; the `|text` serves as verification/relocation fallback) → replace that whole line.
+2. Else literal: run `swissarmyhammer_edit_match::find_match`. On `Unique`, replace that span.
+- Replace semantics follow what `find` resolved to: an anchor find replaces the **line**; a span find replaces the **span**.
+- Safety rule: a structured (anchor) interpretation only **wins** when it resolves; `42:a3` is literal text if line 42 doesn't hash to `a3`. If a resolving anchor **and** a literal match both exist, do not guess — return candidates (handled by the ambiguity task; here just detect and surface).
+- **Atomic batch**: validate/resolve all pairs first, then commit; any failure leaves the file byte-identical. Reuse the existing temp-write+rename (`edit_file_atomic`) / encoding+line-ending preservation — apply all resolved spans in one rewrite.
+- **Idempotency**: reject no-ops (`find == replace`); if a pair's `replace` is already present and `find` is absent, report "likely already applied" rather than hard-erroring.
+- Detect when a later edit's target span was **consumed** by an earlier edit in the same batch and report it specifically.
+- Delete = empty `replace`. Insert = replace a line with itself plus new content (no special op).
+- Keep recording the mutated path via `context.record_mutated_path` so the existing `inline_diagnostics` fold-in still fires (see `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/inline_diagnostics.rs`).
+
+This supersedes the edit/replace split — one op, no separate `replace files`.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] A `N:HH` anchor that resolves replaces that line; a stale anchor falls through to literal text (no mis-apply).
+- [ ] A bare-string `find` is matched via the ladder and the span replaced; normalized-match preserves surrounding bytes/indentation.
+- [ ] Multi-pair batch is atomic: a single failing pair leaves the file byte-identical.
+- [ ] No-op (`find == replace`) is rejected; replace-present/find-absent reports "already applied".
+- [ ] A consumed-target later edit is detected and reported.
+- [ ] `context.record_mutated_path` is still called on success (diagnostics fold-in unaffected).
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Unit/integration tests in `edit/mod.rs`: anchor resolve, stale-anchor-as-literal, normalized span apply, atomic rollback on failure (file byte-identical), idempotency, consumed-target detection.
+- [ ] A test asserting the mutated path is recorded (diagnostics fold-in path).
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-tools edit::` is green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file
